skill.name: biopython
skill.description: A comprehensive toolbox for computational molecular biology; use it when you need programmatic sequence/structure parsing, batch bioinformatics pipelines, or automated NCBI/BLAST workflows.
skill.summary: Provides AI assistants with verified Biopython code patterns for sequence analysis, NCBI queries, and bioinformatics workflows.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The static scanner flagged 538 potential issues, but the vast majority (418 external_commands findings) are Ruby/shell backtick patterns in reference documentation .md files - these are markdown code examples, not executable code. The 11 env_access findings relate to legitimate NCBI Entrez API credential configuration via environment variables. Network and filesystem findings correspond to expected bioinformatics operations (NCBI queries and sequence file I/O). No confirmed malicious patterns, obfuscation, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access for NCBI credentials
low_findings.description: SKILL.md accesses NCBI_EMAIL and NCBI_API_KEY environment variables via os.environ. This is legitimate and required by NCBI Entrez API policy. The skill transparently documents this requirement and recommends against hardcoding secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear evidence of env_access pattern with legitimate bioinformatics use case confirmed by documentation and NCBI API policy.

low_findings.title: Network calls to NCBI services
low_findings.description: SKILL.md and reference files show network calls to NCBI servers for BLAST searches and Entrez database queries. These are expected operations for a bioinformatics skill that interacts with public research databases.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network operations target NCBI public APIs for legitimate bioinformatics workflows. No evidence of data exfiltration to unknown endpoints.

low_findings.title: File system operations for sequence I/O
low_findings.description: SKILL.md and reference files show file operations for reading FASTA/FASTQ/GenBank files and writing BLAST results. These are standard bioinformatics file I/O operations on user-provided data files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File operations read/write user-specified biological sequence files. No access to system files or sensitive directories detected.

content.value_statement: Bioinformatics researchers and students need accurate, efficient code for parsing sequences, querying databases, and building analysis pipelines. This skill provides AI assistants with verified Biopython patterns for sequence analysis, NCBI queries, and automated bioinformatics workflows.
actual_capabilities: Parse and convert biological sequence formats including FASTA, FASTQ, GenBank, and PDB files
Query NCBI databases using Bio.Entrez with proper rate limiting and authentication
Run BLAST searches against NCBI and parse XML results for top hits and metadata
Compute sequence statistics such as GC fraction, molecular weight, and translate codons
Build and analyze phylogenetic trees using distance-based methods and Newick format
Navigate protein structures using the SMCRA hierarchy and compute geometry
limitations: Does not install Biopython or its dependencies automatically
Network-dependent features require internet access to NCBI servers
Large-scale BLAST is better performed with a local BLAST+ installation
Not validated for clinical or diagnostic use cases

use_cases.title: Batch sequence analysis pipeline
use_cases.description: A researcher needs to process hundreds of DNA sequences to compute GC content, translate to proteins, and identify similar sequences in public databases. This skill generates Biopython code that handles batch processing with proper streaming and rate limiting.

use_cases.title: Automated NCBI database queries
use_cases.description: A bioinformatician needs to fetch gene records from NCBI GenBank based on search terms and parse the results for downstream analysis. This skill provides correct Entrez configuration patterns including email and API key setup.

use_cases.title: Protein structure file parsing
use_cases.description: A structural biology student needs to parse PDB files to extract atom coordinates, compute residue distances, and navigate protein chains. This skill generates code using Bio.PDB with the correct SMCRA traversal patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Parse a FASTA file and compute sequence statistics
prompt_templates.prompt: Read a FASTA file containing DNA sequences using Biopython. For each sequence, print the ID, length, and GC fraction. Use SeqIO.parse for streaming to handle large files.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has a FASTA file with unknown sequence count and wants basic statistics for each record.

prompt_templates.title: Search NCBI nucleotide database and fetch records
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Bio.Entrez to search the NCBI nucleotide database for a specific gene name. Fetch the matching GenBank records and extract the accession numbers, organism names, and sequence lengths.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to find and retrieve specific gene sequences from the NCBI database programmatically.

prompt_templates.title: Run a remote BLAST search and parse top hits
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a BLAST search using Bio.Blast.NCBIWWW against the nt database. Parse the XML results with NCBIXML and extract the top 5 alignments with their accession, description, e-value, and score.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has a query sequence and wants to identify similar sequences in the NCBI database.

prompt_templates.title: Build a phylogenetic tree from aligned sequences
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Bio.Phylo and Bio.Phylo.TreeConstruction to read a multiple sequence alignment, compute a distance matrix, and construct a neighbor-joining tree. Draw the tree with labeled branches.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has an alignment file and wants to visualize evolutionary relationships between sequences.

output_examples.input: A FASTA file containing three DNA sequences of varying lengths with a request to compute GC content for each.
output_examples.output: The skill generates code that outputs a table showing each sequence ID, its length in base pairs, the GC fraction as a percentage, and the molecular weight. Sequence records with high AT content are clearly identified.

output_examples.input: A DNA sequence pasted as raw text with a request to find similar sequences via BLAST and retrieve the top match from NCBI.
output_examples.output: The skill generates code that first runs a remote BLAST search, parses the XML result to extract the top hit accession and description, then fetches the full GenBank record for that hit. The output shows the match description, e-value, score, and the aligned region of the top hit.

output_examples.input: A PDB file path with a request to extract residue names and coordinates for chain A.
output_examples.output: The skill generates code using Bio.PDB.PDBParser that navigates the SMCRA hierarchy to access chain A, iterates over residues, and prints each residue name, sequence position, and the coordinates of its alpha carbon atom. Non-standard residues are filtered out using standard amino acid checks.

best_practices: Always set Entrez.email before making NCBI queries as required by NCBI policy
Add delays between NCBI requests to respect rate limits and avoid being blocked
Use streaming parsers (SeqIO.parse) for large files instead of loading everything into memory
anti_patterns: Hardcoding API keys or credentials directly in scripts instead of using environment variables
Making rapid successive NCBI queries without delays, risking IP-based rate limiting
Loading entire large sequence files into memory with SeqIO.read instead of using SeqIO.parse

faq.question: What is Biopython and what can it do?
faq.answer: Biopython is a Python library for computational molecular biology. It provides tools for sequence parsing, NCBI database access, BLAST searches, phylogenetic analysis, and protein structure handling.

faq.question: Does this skill require an internet connection?
faq.answer: Some features need internet access, including NCBI database queries and remote BLAST searches. Local operations like sequence parsing and statistics do not require internet.

faq.question: Do I need NCBI credentials to use this skill?
faq.answer: NCBI requires an email address for Entrez API access. An API key is optional but increases rate limits. Set these as environment variables NCBI_EMAIL and NCBI_API_KEY.

faq.question: Can this skill run BLAST searches locally?
faq.answer: The skill includes patterns for both remote NCBIWWW BLAST and local BLAST. For high-throughput work, a local BLAST+ installation is recommended for better performance.

faq.question: What biological file formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports FASTA, FASTQ, GenBank, PDB, mmCIF, and multiple alignment formats. Use SeqIO for sequence files and AlignIO for alignment files.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for clinical or diagnostic use?
faq.answer: No. This skill is designed for research and educational purposes. It is not validated for clinical diagnostics, patient care, or regulatory-compliant workflows.
